note: Annual breach statistics published by state AGs and federal agencies. mass.gov and atg.wa.gov figures fetched from the official pages; HHS national totals via HIPAA Journal's aggregation of the OCR portal; CISA KEV counts via CISA/SecurityWeek/Cyble. Verify against the linked source before operational use.

sources.label: Massachusetts (OCABR)
sources.summary: 8 years tracked (2019–2026). Latest: 2025 — 2,198 breaches / 3.0M residents. Companies must report breaches affecting MA residents to OCABR.

rows.note: Partial year. Per-company detail in the annual report PDF.
1,909 breaches affecting 609,006 MA residents (law took effect 2007).

sources.label: Washington (AG)
sources.summary: Annual AG reports + a live Socrata API. 2024: 11.6M notices across 279 breaches (500+) — notices first exceeded state population.

rows.note: Jul 2023–Jul 2024. 11.6M notices; cyberattacks were 78% of breaches; SSNs in ~69%.

sources.label: HHS OCR (national)
sources.summary: National HIPAA breaches of 500+. 2024: ~168M individuals across 725 breaches (incl. Change Healthcare ~190M ecosystem).

rows.note: Record year — Change Healthcare alone reached ~190M impacted.
Through Sep 2025 (~43M); year-end higher — Oct 2025 govt shutdown delayed portal postings.

sources.label: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities
sources.summary: Actively-exploited CVEs. 2025: 245 added (24 ransomware-exploited); catalog total 1,484. Tracker pulls the live feed.

rows.note: In progress — live feed (e.g., Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN, Oracle EBS, ASUS Live Update CVE-2025-59374).
245 added (24 known ransomware-exploited, e.g., CitrixBleed 2 CVE-2025-5777). Catalog reached 1,484 (+20%).
185 added; catalog total 1,239 at year end.
